title: feat(code-review): reviews accordion + IA polish (PR detail + list + sidebar)
rationale: multi-domain UI change, has the formatNumber FALSE-positive (round-1 finding [3]) — pure noisy/recurrence target
body:
## Summary

Punch-list items **2** (reviews accordion) and **3** (findings nested under reviews) from #1788, plus a set of IA refinements driven by visual review of the PR detail and list pages, plus a **lens-based 5-section sidebar reorg**.

Stacks on top of #1793 (timeline strip — item 5) once that lands; the two are independent in the same file but touch different sections.

## Highlights

### PR detail page

- Reviews now render as **expandable accordion cards** (one per review). Card head shows: AI mark · Review run #N · skill badge · start→end times · duration · severity-count pills. Newest review auto-expands.
- Findings live **inside each review's body** as card-style rows (severity chip + title/file·line + status pill + disabled \`···\` placeholder for item 4). The global flat findings list and severity/status filter chips are gone.
- H1 no longer prefixes \`#1234\` — breadcrumb has the number, H1 has the title. Breadcrumb relabelled to **\`Pull Requests > #1234\`** to match the new sidebar.

### PR list page

- H1 renamed \`Code Review\` → \`Pull Requests\`.
- **State filter defaults to \`open\`**.
- **Repo filter is now a dropdown** populated from the loaded PRs (the \`/:orgId/repos\` admin endpoint isn't reachable for members, so we derive from visible data).
- Each row redesigned: 4px coloured state rail · \`[state badge] #N title\` + meta · **review-verdict panel** with a **4×4 dot grid** lighting N cells in the worst-severity colour (green for clean reviews) + finding count + review count.
- Standalone severity legend at the bottom is dropped (each row's chip is already colour-coded).
- "actionable" subscript replaced with **\`X FP\`** (false-positive count) — every finding is actionable by definition; what matters is the noise rate.

### Sidebar — 5-section lens-based reorg

Reorganises into five sections, each answering a different mental question:

| Section | Question | Items |
|---|---|---|
| **WORK** | what are we doing? | Epics · Planner · **Pull Requests** · **Review Quality** |
| **EXECUTION** | AI doing things | **New Chat** · **All Conversations** · Workflows · Task Templates |
| **KNOWLEDGE** | the AI's input fabric | Skills · Knowledge Graph · Stack Config |
| **INSIGHTS** | what happened across the org | Overview · Executions · **Skill Usage** · Adoption |
| **ADMIN** | one-off org governance | **Integrations** · **Security** |

**Bold items moved.** Net: 6 → 5 sections, same items, each in a more obvious place. All access gates (\`orgId\` / \`isAdmin\`) preserved exactly — nothing newly hidden or exposed.

Plus icon updates: the Insights \`Code Review\` analytics dashboard is now \`Review Quality\` with a **Gauge icon** (was sharing GitPullRequest with the new Pull Requests entry); fixed vertical alignment of all sidebar icons against their labels (\`.sidebar-icon\` was missing flex-centering — SVG was sitting on the text baseline).

## Out of scope (tracked for follow-ups)

- **Item 4: per-finding \`···\` action menu** (mark TP / mark FP / dismiss) — placeholder button is in place; needs the inline dropdown wired to the existing finding-action API.
- **Item 6: wire \`Run another review\`** — disabled stub on the detail page.
- **Per-severity dot grid** — current grid lights all filled cells in the *worst* severity colour. Painting each cell by its individual finding's severity needs \`findingsBySeverity\` denormalised onto \`pr.stats\` (~half-day backend change).
- **GitLab "Merge Requests" labelling** — the current \`Pull Requests\` label assumes GitHub. A \`// TODO\` comment near the entry references when a primary-provider signal lands.

## Test plan

- [x] \`tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.app.json\` — clean
- [x] \`npm run lint\` — 0 errors
- [x] \`vitest run src/pages/code-review/__tests__/\` — 28/28 pass (8 detail + 8 list + 12 modal)
- [x] Visually verified locally with seeded PR — accordion expand/collapse, dot grid colouring, state stripe, breadcrumb, sidebar reorg + alignment all rendered correctly

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

botReviews.body:
## Code Review — aictrl-dev

**PR #1794** `feat(code-review): reviews accordion + IA polish (PR detail + list + sidebar)` | +790 −499 across 8 files

---

### MAJOR (4)

| # | File | Issue |
|---|------|-------|
| 1 | `CodeReviewPullDetailPage.tsx:540` | **Stale `findingsRef` in `review.completed` SSE handler** — The ref is updated during render, but if `finding-recorded` events arrive in rapid succession before `review.completed`, the ref holds pre-findings state, producing incorrect severity pill counts on the review card. The wrong counts persist until page refresh. Consider computing `findingsBySeverity` server-side in the `ReviewCompletedPayload`, or maintain a `useRef<Map<reviewId, findings[]>>` via functional updater. |
| 2 | `CodeReviewPullDetailPage.tsx:54` & `CodeReviewPullsPage.tsx:50` | **Duplicated `prStateBadgeVariant` function** — Identical logic defined in both page files. Extract to a shared `code-review/utils.ts` to prevent drift. |
| 3 | `CodeReviewPullDetailPage.css:458` & `CodeReviewPullsPage.css:272` | **Dead `.cr-sev-chip` CSS in both files** — These severity chip classes are defined identically in both CSS files and are no longer referenced by any `.tsx` file (replaced by `.crpd-rc-pill--*` and `.cr-pr-fgrid--*`). Remove from both locations. |
| 4 | `Sidebar.tsx:267` & `Sidebar.tsx:309` | **Two sidebar sections share the heading "Admin"** — One is always-visible (Integrations, Security), the other is `orgId && isAdmin`-gated (Dashboard, Members, Org Settings). Admin users see two identical section headings stacked. Rename the first to "Account" or merge into the gated section. |

### MINOR (3)

| # | File | Issue |
|---|------|-------|
| 5 | `CodeReviewPullDetailPage.css:33` | **Dead `.crpd-pr-num` class** — Defined but never used after the `#` prefix was removed from the H1. Remove the rule. |
| 6 | `CodeReviewPullDetailPage.tsx:428` | **Unmemoized `findings.filter()` per review** — `findings.filter((f) => f.reviewId === review.id)` runs inside `.map()` without memoization, O(R×F) per render. Build a `useMemo`-ed `Map<reviewId, findings[]>` once. |
| 7 | `CodeReviewPullsPage.css:266` | **Dead `.cr-severity-dots` and `.cr-no-findings` classes** — Remnants of the old row design. Remove both rules. |

### NIT (1)

| # | File | Issue |
|---|------|-------|
| 8 | `CodeReviewPullsPage.tsx:121` | **Dynamic CSS class `cr-pr-row--state-${pr.state}` lacks fallback** — Only `open`, `merged`, `closed` have rail styles. If the API adds a new state, the rail silently falls back to the muted default. Consider normalizing unknown states. |

---

Reviewed SHA: 138cda5dd7a57d68c56ac056175863111c343ef2

Dynamic class cr-pr-row--state-${state} lacks fallback for unknown states
findings.reason: state enum is well-defined union; muted default rail is fine for unknown — stylistic

findings.description: Severity pill backgrounds use hardcoded rgba() values instead of CSS custom properties
findings.reason: design-system-token-blocked — no --severity-*-bg token family exists yet; tracked as a follow-up
alpha lowered to 0.12 to match every other severity/status background in this file; explanatory comment added re the missing --status-warning-bg token
